commands.review_summary:
The PR recomputes known-priced nonzero-token usage when transcripts record `usage.cost.total: 0`, bumps the usage-cost cache version, aligns session-log cost display, and hydrates all-zero DeepSeek V4 configured costs from the bundled catalog.

PR surface: Source +37, Tests +184. Total +221 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main preserves a recorded `cost.total: 0` for nonzero usage when flat model pricing is known. I did not run a live DeepSeek API or Control UI session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor proof and maintainer compatibility judgment are needed before merge; there is no narrow automation repair to queue from this review.

target.title: fix(deepseek): recover V4 zero-cost spend
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #97047.

DeepSeek V4 usage can still surface as zero spend through two related paths:

- session transcripts may contain nonzero token usage with `usage.cost.total: 0`, which should be recomputed when the model has known positive pricing;
- user-configured DeepSeek V4 model rows can carry an all-zero `cost` object, which previously counted as an explicit cost override and blocked hydration from the bundled DeepSeek catalog.

That combination can leave Control UI Spend and session log views reporting known-priced DeepSeek V4 usage as zero.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change keeps the generic session-cost guard from preserving recorded zero totals for known-priced nonzero-token usage, clears stale cost breakdowns so aggregation uses the recomputed estimate, and bumps the usage-cost cache version so older cached zero totals are rebuilt.

It also tightens the DeepSeek provider policy: catalog pricing now hydrates when the configured model has no positive cost values, while preserving explicit positive user cost overrides.

## User Impact

DeepSeek V4 spend summaries, cache rebuilds, and session logs now show estimated cost for known-priced token usage instead of a fabricated zero. DeepSeek V4 model configs with all-zero cost placeholders recover the bundled catalog pricing automatically.

## Evidence

- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` passed locally on the PR branch.
- Added fixture coverage in `src/infra/session-cost-usage.test.ts` for known-priced DeepSeek V4 recorded zero cost, including summary totals, cache upgrade behavior, and `loadSessionLogs()` cost display.
- Added fixture coverage in `extensions/deepseek/provider-policy-api.test.ts` for all-zero DeepSeek V4 cost hydration from catalog pricing.
- Broader runtime validation should come from GitHub Actions on this PR head; no local broad test/build was run.

## Related Work

PR #97065 covers the core session-cost aggregation path for the same issue. This PR additionally covers DeepSeek provider catalog hydration for all-zero cost placeholders and keeps the session-log behavior aligned with the spend summary path.
